summary: Reusable settings row for editable, read-only, locked, pending, disabled, and degraded setting values. The row keeps the setting label, current value, source, lock explanation, and next safe action visible without collapsing policy state into ordinary disabled styling.

hook_records.notes: Reference theme row for settings review.
High-contrast review keeps locked, read-only, pending, and disabled states distinct without hue-only reliance.
Settings rows inherit live appearance, density, contrast, and forced-colors changes.

slots.description: Current effective value or read-only snapshot summary.
Locked, read-only, pending, degraded, disabled, or invalid state badge.
Policy, trust, source, read-only scope, degraded capability, or disabled reason.
Inline control or route that edits the setting when the state allows it.
Reset or revert action when a local override exists and policy allows it.
Route to effective setting, policy, source, or schema documentation.

responsive_reflow_notes: Setting label and value summary remain visible before optional actions.
Source explanation may wrap under the value summary but never moves to hover-only disclosure.
State badge stays adjacent to the value summary in compact and standard density so locked and read-only states remain scannable.

state_nodes.user_visible_summary: The setting row is readable and can enter edit mode when the inline control is present.
Pointer hover adds emphasis without revealing unique setting state or source information.
Keyboard focus and current detail-owner state are active on this row.
The setting can be inspected and copied but not edited in the current scope.
An organization policy enforces the value and blocks local edits or reset.
The user changed the setting and the value is staged until apply, sync, or restart completes.
The row shows the last truthful value while policy or sync source freshness is reduced.
The current or staged value is invalid and needs review before it can apply.
The edit control is unavailable because the setting has no applicable action in this context.

transitions.user_visible_effect: Focus ring moves to the row and the row becomes the current detail owner.
Pending badge and source explanation name the staged value and apply condition.
Pending state clears and the row keeps focus and current context.
Lock badge and policy source replace edit affordances without hiding the effective value.
Lock explanation clears and edit controls return without moving focus.
The row names the last truthful value and the reduced freshness source.
The row preserves value contrast and names the read-only scope.
The edit control is unavailable with a direct reason rather than a policy or read-only explanation.

slot_content_rules.notes: The setting name remains visible before badges or actions.
The effective value remains readable in default, read-only, locked, and degraded states.
Badges use taxonomy labels such as Locked, Read-only, Pending, Degraded, Warning, or Disabled.
Policy, source, read-only scope, disabled reason, or degraded freshness explanation stays inline.
Edit action names the exact setting mutation or opens a fuller editor.
Reset hides only when no local override or writable route exists.
Help link routes to policy, source stack, or setting schema details.

content_contract.blocked_and_degraded_copy_rule: Locked copy names policy or authority source; read-only copy names inspectable scope; disabled copy names ordinary context unavailability; degraded copy names the last truthful value, what is reduced, and how to refresh.
content_contract.empty_or_missing_data_rule: If source or value data is missing, the row says which source could not be resolved and keeps any known effective value separate from the missing detail.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move focus to the next settings row.
primary_navigation_keys.when: While focus remains in the settings list.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move focus to the previous settings row.
primary_navigation_keys.when: While focus remains in the settings list.

primary_navigation_keys.effect: Move from the row to edit, reset, and help affordances in stable order.
primary_navigation_keys.when: Only after the row itself owns focus.

activation_keys.effect: Open the setting detail or edit route when the row is not locked, disabled, or read-only.
Activate the focused inline control or open the current row detail.
activation_keys.when: When an inline control owns focus or the row advertises a default detail route.

interaction_contract.focus_return_rule: Closing a setting detail, source explanation, or policy sheet returns focus to the originating row unless the setting was removed, in which case focus moves to the nearest surviving row.
interaction_contract.pointer_behavior_note: Hover may increase emphasis but must not reveal the only policy source, read-only scope, pending condition, or degraded explanation.

accessibility_contract.role_description: Settings row with optional inline controls.
accessibility_contract.accessible_name_rule: Accessible name starts with the setting label and appends the current value and state badge when the badge materially changes actionability.
accessibility_contract.accessible_description_rule: Accessible description appends source explanation, read-only scope, policy source, pending condition, or degraded freshness in stable order.

announcement_rules.message_rule: Locked state appends the policy source and available help route without moving focus.
Pending state announces the staged change and apply condition once.
Degraded source state announces that the row is using the last truthful value and names the refresh route.

accessibility_contract.high_contrast_behavior: Locked, read-only, pending, disabled, and degraded states use text, icon, and border changes so high-contrast themes do not rely on hue.
accessibility_contract.forced_colors_behavior: Forced-colors mode preserves focus, current row, lock cue, and read-only cue through system color borders and text labels.
accessibility_contract.blocked_and_degraded_behavior: Locked, read-only, disabled, and degraded rows remain focusable and explain why editing is blocked or narrowed in the accessibility tree.

semantic_token_bindings.notes: Current focused row stays distinct from selected batch membership.
Policy lock uses trust and warning semantics, not disabled opacity.
Read-only, disabled, and degraded explanations remain legible and distinct.
Pending uses submitted-action feedback rather than initial loading treatment.

density_variant_bindings.layout_effect: Compact layout keeps label, value, and badge on the first line when possible; source explanation wraps beneath.
Comfortable layout allows longer source and policy explanations without changing focus order.

motion_bindings.reduced_motion_behavior: State badge and explanation changes become instant; no shimmer or animated lock transition survives reduced-motion postures.

state_visualization_bindings.notes: Current row remains distinct from selected batch state.
Read-only preserves inspectability and names scope.
Locked is source-explained and not treated as ordinary disabled.
Pending names the staged user action and apply condition.
Degraded names the last truthful value and source-freshness impact.
Disabled names ordinary unavailable context and does not carry a lock source.

asset_slots.notes: State badge uses semantic status icons including lock or read-only glyphs where relevant.
Edit control may use a standard shell edit icon with accessible text.
Help link icon may mirror only when directionally meaningful.

localization_contract.text_expansion_strategy: Label and badge stay visible first; value and source explanation wrap before actions collapse into an overflow route.
localization_contract.bidi_behavior: Setting keys or policy identifiers inside explanatory copy are isolated while surrounding text follows the active locale direction.
localization_contract.formatting_behavior: Effective dates, source epochs, restart conditions, and policy bundle names use locale-aware formatting where they are not raw identifiers.
localization_contract.source_language_fallback_behavior: If a policy source label is available only in the source language, the row preserves it and adds translated framing instead of inventing a local substitute.
localization_contract.notes: Pseudoloc expansion must not hide source or lock explanations.

allowed_extension_variations: An extension settings surface may omit reset_action when the host declares no reset route.
An extension settings surface may add one provider badge when it preserves the shared taxonomy state badge.
extension_guidance.required_gap_disclosure: If an extension cannot distinguish locked, read-only, pending, degraded, disabled, and current states, it must disclose the gap and hand off to the host settings row.

substitution_and_deprecation.unsupported_package_behavior: If a theme or extension package cannot express locked, read-only, pending, degraded, and disabled as distinct states, the host row falls back to first-party state treatment and exposes the mismatch in settings diagnostics.
